words.definitionEn: a formal suggestion or plan; the act of making a suggestion
words.exampleEn: to submit/present/put forward a proposal

words.definitionEn: to suggest a plan, an idea, etc. for people to think about and decide on
words.exampleEn: propose something, The government proposed changes to the voting system.

words.definitionEn: making you feel embarrassed
words.exampleEn: There was an awkward silence.

words.definitionEn: the possibility that something will happen
words.exampleEn: prospect of something, There is no immediate prospect of peace.

words.definitionEn: the act of protecting somebody/something; the state of being protected
words.exampleEn: protection against something, Wear clothes that provide adequate protection against the wind and rain.

words.definitionEn: a substance, found within all living things, that forms the structure of muscles, organs, etc. There are many different proteins and they are an essential part of what humans and animals eat to help them grow and stay healthy.
words.exampleEn: essential proteins and vitamins

words.definitionEn: a person who makes a public protest
words.exampleEn: Thousands of protesters marched through the city.

words.definitionEn: connected with a person’s mind and the way in which it works
words.exampleEn: the psychological development of children

words.definitionEn: a scientist who studies and is trained in psychology
words.exampleEn: to see a psychologist

words.definitionEn: the scientific study of the mind and how it influences behaviour
words.exampleEn: clinical/educational/child/sport psychology

words.definitionEn: the act of printing a book, a magazine, etc. and making it available to the public; a book, a magazine, etc. that has been published
words.exampleEn: specialist publications

words.definitionEn: the attention that is given to somebody/something by newspapers, television, etc.
words.exampleEn: good/bad/adverse publicity

words.definitionEn: the profession or business of preparing and printing books, magazines, CD-ROMs, etc. and selling or making them available to the public
words.exampleEn: a job in publishing

words.definitionEn: a type of loud and aggressive rock music popular in the late 1970s and early 1980s
words.exampleEn: a punk band

words.definitionEn: a person who is being taught, especially a child in a school
words.exampleEn: school pupils

words.definitionEn: the act or process of buying something
words.exampleEn: to make a purchase (= buy something)

words.definitionEn: to buy something
words.exampleEn: purchase something, Please ensure that you purchase your ticket in advance.

words.definitionEn: not mixed with anything else; with nothing added
words.exampleEn: pure gold

words.definitionEn: only; completely
words.exampleEn: I saw the letter purely by chance.

words.definitionEn: to do something or try to achieve something over a period of time
words.exampleEn: to pursue a goal

words.definitionEn: to encourage somebody or give them help; to give financial support to somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Her parents backed her in her choice of career.

words.definitionEn: the act of looking for or trying to get something
words.exampleEn: pursuit of something, the pursuit of happiness/knowledge/profit

words.definitionEn: a game, etc. that you have to think about carefully in order to answer it or do it
words.exampleEn: a crossword puzzle

words.definitionEn: a written list of questions that are answered by a number of people so that information can be collected from the answers
words.exampleEn: (British English), to fill in a questionnaire

words.definitionEn: happening or existing between people of different races
words.exampleEn: racial hatred/prejudice/tension/violence

words.definitionEn: the unfair treatment of people who belong to a different race; violent behaviour towards them
words.exampleEn: a victim of racism

words.definitionEn: having the belief that some races of people are better than others or having general beliefs about other people based only on their race; showing this through violent or unfair treatment of people of other races
words.exampleEn: racist attitudes/remarks

words.definitionEn: a person who believes that some races of people are better than others or who has general beliefs about other people based only on their race, and may show this in violent or unfair treatment of people of other races
words.exampleEn: He's a racist.

words.definitionEn: powerful and very dangerous rays that are sent out from radioactive substances
words.exampleEn: high levels/doses of radiation that damage cells

words.definitionEn: a wooden or metal bar placed around something as a barrier or to provide support
words.exampleEn: She leaned on the ship's rail and gazed out to sea.

words.definitionEn: done, chosen, etc. without somebody deciding in advance what is going to happen, or without any regular pattern
words.exampleEn: the random killing of innocent people

words.definitionEn: to vary between two particular amounts, sizes, etc., including others between them
words.exampleEn: range from A to B, Accommodation ranges from tourist class to luxury hotels.

words.definitionEn: the position, especially a high position, that somebody has in a particular organization, society, etc.
words.exampleEn: She was not used to mixing with people of high social rank.

words.definitionEn: to give somebody/something a particular position on a scale according to quality, importance, success, etc.; to have a position of this kind
words.exampleEn: rank somebody/something, In most Australian elections, electors are required to rank all candidates.

words.definitionEn: happening quickly or in a short period of time
words.exampleEn: rapid change/expansion/growth/development

words.definitionEn: very quickly; at a great rate
words.exampleEn: to increase/spread/expand rapidly

words.definitionEn: the simplest and smallest forms of life. Bacteria exist in large numbers in air, water and soil, and also in living and dead creatures and plants, and are often a cause of disease.
words.exampleEn: Neither chilling nor freezing kills all bacteria.

words.definitionEn: a small animal with a long tail, that looks like a large mouse, usually considered a pest (= an animal which is disliked because it destroys food or spreads disease)
words.exampleEn: rat poison

words.definitionEn: to have or think that somebody/something has a particular level of quality, value, etc.
words.exampleEn: rate somebody/something + adv./prep., The university is highly rated for its research.

words.definitionEn: a measurement of how good, popular, important, etc. somebody/something is, especially in relation to other people or things
words.exampleEn: The poll gave an approval rating of 39 per cent for the president.

words.definitionEn: not cooked
words.exampleEn: raw meat

words.definitionEn: the distance over which you can stretch your arms to touch something; the distance over which a particular object can be used to touch something else
words.exampleEn: beyond somebody's reach, The shot was well beyond the reach of the goalkeeper.

words.definitionEn: a small piece of metal or plastic, with a design or words on it, that a person wears or carries to show that they belong to an organization, support something, have achieved something, have a particular rank, etc.
words.exampleEn: She wore a badge saying ‘Vote for Coates’.

words.definitionEn: accepting in a sensible way what it is actually possible to do or achieve in a particular situation
words.exampleEn: a realistic assessment/approach/view

words.definitionEn: fair, practical, and sensible
words.exampleEn: it is reasonable to do something, It is reasonable to assume that he knew beforehand that this would happen.

words.definitionEn: to a degree that is fairly good but not very good
words.exampleEn: The instructions are reasonably straightforward.

words.definitionEn: to build or put something together again
words.exampleEn: After the earthquake, the people set about rebuilding their homes.

words.definitionEn: to remember something
words.exampleEn: recall something, She could not recall his name.

words.definitionEn: the part of a phone that you hold close to your mouth and ear
words.exampleEn: to pick up/lift/put down/replace the receiver

words.definitionEn: a difficult time for the economy of a country, when there is less trade and industrial activity than usual and more people are unemployed
words.exampleEn: How do you assess the impact of the current recession on manufacturing?

words.definitionEn: to think something or have an opinion about something
words.exampleEn: I reckon (that) I’m going to get that job.

words.definitionEn: the act of remembering who somebody is when you see them, or of identifying what something is
words.exampleEn: He glanced briefly towards her but there was no sign of recognition.

words.definitionEn: to get well again after being ill, hurt, etc.
words.exampleEn: recover from something, He's still recovering from his operation.

words.definitionEn: the process of becoming well again after an illness or injury
words.exampleEn: My father has made a full recovery from the operation.

words.definitionEn: a person who has recently joined the armed forces or the police
words.exampleEn: the training of new recruits

words.definitionEn: to have the opinion that somebody/something is a particular thing
words.exampleEn: be accounted + adj., In English law a person is accounted innocent until they are proved guilty.

words.definitionEn: to find new people to join a company, an organization, the armed forces, etc.
words.exampleEn: recruit (somebody), We are trying to recruit officers from more diverse backgrounds.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of finding new people to join a company, an organization, the armed forces, etc.
words.exampleEn: the recruitment of new members

words.definitionEn: an act of making something less or smaller; the state of being made less or smaller
words.exampleEn: Some staff received wage rises, while others experienced a reduction.

words.definitionEn: the official who controls the game in some sports
words.exampleEn: He was sent off for arguing with the referee.

words.definitionEn: a person who has been forced to leave their country or home, because there is a war or for political, religious or social reasons
words.exampleEn: There has been a steady flow of refugees from the war zone.

words.definitionEn: attention to or thought and care for somebody/something
words.exampleEn: regard for somebody/something, to have scant/little/no regard for somebody/something

words.definitionEn: to think about somebody/something in a particular way
words.exampleEn: regard somebody/something + adv./prep., They regarded people outside their own town with suspicion.

words.definitionEn: of or relating to a region
words.exampleEn: services available at a local and regional level

words.definitionEn: an official list or record of names, items, etc.; a book that contains such a list
words.exampleEn: The bride and groom signed the register.

words.definitionEn: to record your/somebody’s/something’s name on an official list
words.exampleEn: You can also register online.

words.definitionEn: the act of making an official record of something/somebody
words.exampleEn: the registration of letters and parcels

words.definitionEn: a sad feeling because of something that has happened or something that you have done or not done
words.exampleEn: a feeling/pang/twinge of regret

words.definitionEn: keeping or showing a balance so that different things or different parts of something exist in equal or correct amounts
words.exampleEn: The programme presented a balanced view of the two sides of the conflict.

words.definitionEn: to feel sorry about something you have done or about something that you have not been able to do
words.exampleEn: regret something, If you don't do it now, you'll only regret it.

words.definitionEn: to control something by means of rules
words.exampleEn: The department is responsible for regulating the insurance industry.

words.definitionEn: an official rule made by a government or some other authority
words.exampleEn: too many rules and regulations

words.definitionEn: to make a feeling, an idea, etc. stronger
words.exampleEn: The experience reinforced my sense of loss.

words.definitionEn: to a fairly large degree, especially in comparison to something else
words.exampleEn: relatively large/small/high/low

words.definitionEn: a style of dancing that tells a dramatic story with music but no talking or singing
words.exampleEn: She wants to be a ballet dancer.

words.definitionEn: closely connected with the subject you are discussing or the situation you are in
words.exampleEn: relevant information/facts/documents/factors

words.definitionEn: the feeling of happiness that you have when something unpleasant stops or does not happen
words.exampleEn: I felt a huge sense of relief when I heard they were all OK.

words.definitionEn: a small bag made of very thin rubber that becomes larger and rounder when you fill it with air or gas. Balloons are brightly coloured and used as decorations or toys.
words.exampleEn: to blow up/burst/pop a balloon

words.definitionEn: to remove or reduce an unpleasant feeling or pain
words.exampleEn: to relieve the symptoms of a cold

words.definitionEn: feeling happy because something unpleasant has stopped or has not happened; showing this
words.exampleEn: She sounded relieved.

words.definitionEn: something that you say or write which expresses an opinion, a thought, etc. about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: to make a remark

words.definitionEn: to say or write a comment about something/somebody
words.exampleEn: Sport, George Orwell once remarked, is like war.

words.definitionEn: unusual or surprising in a way that causes people to take notice
words.exampleEn: a remarkable achievement/career/talent

words.definitionEn: in a way that is unusual or surprising and causes people to take notice
words.exampleEn: The car is in remarkably good condition for its age.

words.definitionEn: the presenting of and writing about news on television and radio, and in newspapers
words.exampleEn: accurate/balanced/objective reporting

words.definitionEn: typical of a particular group of people; that is a typical example of something
words.exampleEn: Is a questionnaire answered by 500 people truly representative of the population as a whole?

words.definitionEn: a person who has been chosen to speak or vote for somebody else or for a group of people, or to take the place of somebody else
words.exampleEn: Our elected representatives in government should be doing something about this.

words.definitionEn: the opinion that people have about what somebody/something is like, based on what has happened in the past
words.exampleEn: to have a good/bad reputation

words.definitionEn: something that you need or want
words.exampleEn: the basic requirements of life

words.definitionEn: the act of saving somebody/something from a dangerous or difficult situation; the fact of being saved
words.exampleEn: We had given up hope of rescue.

words.definitionEn: to save somebody/something from a dangerous or harmful situation
words.exampleEn: rescue somebody/something from something/somebody, The coastguard rescued six people from the sinking boat.

words.definitionEn: a supply of something that is available to be used in the future or when it is needed
words.exampleEn: cash/foreign currency reserves

words.definitionEn: to ask for a seat, table, room, etc. to be available for you or somebody else at a future time
words.exampleEn: reserve something for somebody/something, I'd like to reserve a table for three for eight o'clock.

words.definitionEn: living in a particular place
words.exampleEn: the town’s resident population (= not tourists or visitors)

words.definitionEn: a person who lives in a particular place or who has their home there
words.exampleEn: a resident of the United States

words.definitionEn: to officially tell somebody that you are leaving your job, an organization, etc.
words.exampleEn: She was forced to resign due to ill health.

words.definitionEn: to refuse to accept something and try to stop it from happening
words.exampleEn: resist something, They are determined to resist pressure to change the law.

words.definitionEn: a definite decision to do or not to do something
words.exampleEn: Have you made any New Year's resolutions (= for example, to give up smoking from 1 January)?

words.definitionEn: to find an acceptable solution to a problem or difficulty
words.exampleEn: resolve something, to resolve an issue/a dispute/a conflict/a crisis

words.definitionEn: a place where a lot of people go on holiday
words.exampleEn: seaside/mountain resorts

words.definitionEn: to bring back a situation or feeling that existed before
words.exampleEn: restore something, The measures are intended to restore public confidence in the economy.

words.definitionEn: to limit the size, amount or range of something
words.exampleEn: restrict something to something, Speed is restricted to 30 mph in towns.

words.definitionEn: to ban or prevent somebody from doing something
words.exampleEn: bar somebody from doing something, Prisoners are barred by law from voting in general elections.

words.definitionEn: a rule or law that limits what you can do or what can happen
words.exampleEn: import/speed/travel restrictions

words.definitionEn: the selling of goods to the public, usually through shops
words.exampleEn: The recommended retail price is £9.99.

words.definitionEn: to keep something; to continue to have something
words.exampleEn: to retain your independence

words.definitionEn: the fact of leaving your job and stopping work, usually because you have reached a particular age; the time when you do this
words.exampleEn: At 60, he was now approaching retirement.

words.definitionEn: to make something known to somebody
words.exampleEn: reveal something, to reveal a secret

words.definitionEn: the money that a government receives from taxes or that an organization, etc. receives from its business
words.exampleEn: The government is currently facing a shortfall in tax revenue.

words.definitionEn: in a way that is just possible but only with difficulty
words.exampleEn: He could barely read and write.

words.definitionEn: a change or set of changes to something
words.exampleEn: He made some minor revisions to the report before printing it out.

words.definitionEn: an attempt, by a large number of people, to change the government of a country, especially by violent action
words.exampleEn: a socialist revolution

words.definitionEn: a thing that you are given because you have done something good, worked hard, etc.
words.exampleEn: a financial reward

words.definitionEn: a thing bought for less than the usual price
words.exampleEn: I picked up a few good bargains in the sale.

words.definitionEn: to give something to somebody because they have done something good, worked hard, etc.
words.exampleEn: reward somebody for something, She was rewarded for her efforts with a cash bonus.

words.definitionEn: a strong regular repeated pattern of sounds or movements
words.exampleEn: to dance to the rhythm of the music

words.definitionEn: to be free of somebody/something that has been annoying you or that you do not want
words.exampleEn: She wanted to be rid of her parents and their authority.

words.definitionEn: very silly or unreasonable
words.exampleEn: I look ridiculous in this hat.

words.definitionEn: an object like a fence that prevents people from moving forward from one place to another
words.exampleEn: The crowd had to stand behind barriers.

words.definitionEn: involving the possibility of something bad happening
words.exampleEn: Life as an aid worker can be a risky business (= dangerous).

words.definitionEn: competing with another person, company, thing, etc.
words.exampleEn: a rival bid/claim/offer

words.definitionEn: a person, company or thing that competes with another in sport, business, etc.
words.exampleEn: The two teams have always been rivals.

words.definitionEn: to steal money or property from a person or place
words.exampleEn: to rob a bank

words.definitionEn: the crime of stealing money or goods from a bank, shop, person, etc., especially using violence or threats
words.exampleEn: armed robbery (= using a gun, knife, etc.)

words.definitionEn: a spacecraft in the shape of a tube that is driven by a stream of gases let out behind it when fuel is burned inside
words.exampleEn: a space rocket

words.definitionEn: an exciting, usually short, relationship between two people who are in love with each other
words.exampleEn: a holiday romance

words.definitionEn: the part of a plant that grows under the ground and takes in water and minerals that it sends to the rest of the plant
words.exampleEn: deep spreading roots

words.definitionEn: a flower with a sweet smell that grows on a bush with thorns (= sharp points) on its stems
words.exampleEn: a bunch of red roses

words.definitionEn: approximately but not exactly
words.exampleEn: Sales are up by roughly 10 per cent.

words.definitionEn: a stage in a competition or sports event
words.exampleEn: the qualifying rounds of the National Championships

words.definitionEn: done or happening as a normal part of a particular job, situation or process
words.exampleEn: routine enquiries/questions/tests/screening

words.definitionEn: to move your hand, a cloth, etc., backwards and forwards over a surface while pressing it
words.exampleEn: rub something, He sat up on the hard bunk and rubbed his eyes.

words.definitionEn: made of rubber
words.exampleEn: a rubber ball

words.definitionEn: a strong substance that can be stretched and does not allow liquids to pass through it, used for making tyres, boots, etc. It is made from the liquid (= sap) inside a tropical plant or is produced using chemicals.
words.exampleEn: a ball made of rubber
